# NIGHT COUNTER

- written by: Mira Quell
- draft date: 2026-02-20
- email: mira.quell@example.com
- phone: +1 (310) 555-0137
- address: 4128 Helios Ave, Apt 5B, Los Angeles, CA 90027

## INT. DINER - NIGHT

Rain needles the neon sign while the lunch counter buzzes.
Outside, buses hiss to a stop and pull away half-empty.

> @Maya
> (low)
> If he calls, tell him I was never here.
> Tell him you checked every booth.
> Tell him the coffee machine never stopped and nobody heard a thing.
> Tell him the night shift changed at midnight and I left before the weather broke.
> Tell him the receipt printer jammed and erased the timestamp.
> Tell him the camera over the register blinked out for routine maintenance.
> Tell him the alley door stuck like it always does when it rains.
> Tell him the delivery truck blocked the rear view.
> Tell him you never saw the envelope.
> Tell him you never saw me open it.
> Tell him you never saw the map inside.
> Tell him you do not know what station that code belongs to.
> Tell him this city has too many tunnels and not enough exits.
> Tell him if he wants to find me he should start where the lights go out first.

The waiter refills her coffee and slides a folded note across the counter.

> @Waiter
> You dropped this by the door.

### CUT TO:

## EXT. ALLEY BEHIND THE DINER - CONTINUOUS

A black sedan idles in the dark with headlights off.
